---
title: "Command Core Types & Factory"
description: "Core type definitions and factory function for the command architecture"
---

## Overview

The command core types and factory provide the foundational infrastructure for the
[Command Architecture](index.qmd). These are the building blocks that all concrete commands
and command consumers depend on.

**Source files:**

- `src/Commands/Core/CommandResult.hpp` — Result type for command execution
- `src/Commands/Core/CommandContext.hpp` — Runtime context provided to commands
- `src/Commands/Core/CommandDescriptor.hpp` — JSON-serializable command descriptors
- `src/Commands/Core/ICommand.hpp` — Abstract base class for all commands
- `src/Commands/Core/CommandFactory.hpp` / `.cpp` — Thin wrappers delegating to `CommandRegistry`
- `src/Commands/Core/CommandRegistry.hpp` / `.cpp` — Singleton registry and `registerTypedCommand<>`

All types live in the `commands` namespace and have no Qt dependency.

## Core Types

### CommandResult

Returned by every command's `execute()` and `undo()` methods:

```cpp
struct CommandResult {
    bool success = false;
    std::string error_message;
    std::vector<std::string> affected_keys;

    static CommandResult ok(std::vector<std::string> keys = {});
    static CommandResult error(std::string msg);
};
```

### CommandContext

Runtime environment passed to commands. Not serialized — constructed at execution time:

```cpp
struct CommandContext {
    std::shared_ptr<DataManager> data_manager;
    std::map<std::string, std::string> runtime_variables;
    std::function<void(float)> on_progress;
};
```

### CommandDescriptor

JSON-serializable description of a single command invocation. Uses reflect-cpp for
serialization:

```cpp
struct CommandDescriptor {
    std::string command_name;
    std::optional<rfl::Generic> parameters;
    std::optional<std::string> description;
};
```

### CommandSequenceDescriptor

A sequence of commands with optional template variables for `${variable}` substitution:

```cpp
struct CommandSequenceDescriptor {
    std::optional<std::string> name;
    std::optional<std::string> version;
    std::optional<std::map<std::string, std::string>> variables;
    std::vector<CommandDescriptor> commands;
};
```

## ICommand Base Class

All commands inherit from `ICommand`. The interface provides:

- `execute()` — pure virtual, performs the command's action
- `commandName()` — pure virtual, returns a human-readable name
- `toJson()` — pure virtual, serializes parameters to JSON
- `isUndoable()` — opt-in, defaults to `false`
- `undo()` — opt-in, defaults to returning an error
- `mergeId()` / `mergeWith()` — for undo coalescing (Phase 5)

There is no separate `IUndoableCommand` hierarchy. Commands opt into undo support by overriding
`isUndoable()` and `undo()`.

## Command Factory & Registry

`CommandRegistry` is the runtime lookup table. Libraries register commands at startup via
`registerTypedCommand<>` in explicit dispatcher functions (see
[Command Registry](command_registry.qmd)).

`CommandFactory` exposes backward-compatible free functions that delegate to the registry:

```cpp
std::unique_ptr<ICommand> createCommand(
    std::string const & name,
    rfl::Generic const & params);

std::vector<CommandInfo> getAvailableCommands();
std::optional<CommandInfo> getCommandInfo(std::string const & name);
```

`createCommand()` returns `nullptr` if the command name is unknown or parameter deserialization
fails.

## Tests

Test file: `src/Commands/Core/CommandFactory.test.cpp`

Tests cover:

- `CommandResult` factory methods (`ok`, `error`)
- `CommandDescriptor` and `CommandSequenceDescriptor` JSON round-trip serialization
- Factory returns `nullptr` for unknown and empty command names
- `ICommand` default behavior (not undoable, undo returns error, merge disabled)
- Concrete `StubCommand` execution and name reporting

## See Also

- [Command Architecture](index.qmd) — full design document
- [Variable Substitution & Sequence Execution](sequence_execution.qmd) — runtime execution engine
- [Command Architecture Roadmap](roadmap.qmd) — implementation plan
